description: Zenvyra Restaurant Management System - semantic layer for conversational analytics

total_revenue.description: Total revenue from all completed orders (excludes cancelled/open)
total_revenue.sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

total_revenue_with_tax.description: Total revenue including tax from completed orders
total_revenue_with_tax.sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount + tax_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

total_gross_revenue.description: Total gross revenue including tax and tips from completed orders
total_gross_revenue.sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount + tax_amount + tip_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

total_tips.description: Total tips collected from completed orders
total_tips.sql: SELECT SUM(tip_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

total_tax_collected.description: Total tax collected from completed orders
total_tax_collected.sql: SELECT SUM(tax_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

average_order_value.sql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

total_orders.description: Total number of orders placed (all statuses)

open_orders.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'open'

completed_orders.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

cancelled_orders.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled'

cancellation_rate.description: Percentage of orders that were cancelled
cancellation_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'cancelled') / COUNT(*), 2) FROM orders

revenue_by_location.description: Total revenue broken down by restaurant location
revenue_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN location l ON o.location_id = l.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY revenue DESC

revenue_by_day.description: Daily revenue trend for completed orders
revenue_by_day.sql: SELECT DATE(order_time) AS date, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY DATE(order_time) ORDER BY date

revenue_by_month.description: Monthly revenue trend for completed orders
revenue_by_month.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_time) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

orders_by_employee.description: Number of completed orders handled per employee
orders_by_employee.sql: SELECT e.name AS employee, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_sales FROM orders o JOIN employee e ON o.employee_id = e.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY e.name ORDER BY total_sales DESC

revenue_by_hour.description: Revenue breakdown by hour of day to identify peak trading hours
revenue_by_hour.sql: SELECT EXTRACT(HOUR FROM order_time) AS hour, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

menu.description: Menu items, categories and pricing performance

active_menu_items.description: Number of currently available menu items
active_menu_items.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM menu_items WHERE is_active = true

inactive_menu_items.description: Number of menu items currently unavailable
inactive_menu_items.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM menu_items WHERE is_active = false

menu_items_by_category.description: Count of menu items per category
menu_items_by_category.sql: SELECT mc.name AS category, COUNT(mi.id) AS item_count FROM menu_items mi JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id GROUP BY mc.name ORDER BY item_count DESC

top_selling_items.description: Top 10 best-selling menu items by total quantity ordered
top_selling_items.sql: SELECT mi.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN menu_items mi ON oi.menu_item_id = mi.id GROUP BY mi.name ORDER BY total_sold DESC LIMIT 10

revenue_by_menu_item.description: Revenue generated per menu item from completed orders
revenue_by_menu_item.sql: SELECT mi.name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN menu_items mi ON oi.menu_item_id = mi.id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY mi.name ORDER BY revenue DESC

revenue_by_category.description: Revenue broken down by menu category
revenue_by_category.sql: SELECT mc.name AS category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN menu_items mi ON oi.menu_item_id = mi.id JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY mc.name ORDER BY revenue DESC

average_item_price.description: Average base price across all active menu items
average_item_price.sql: SELECT AVG(base_price) FROM menu_items WHERE is_active = true

price_range_by_category.description: Min and max price per menu category for active items
price_range_by_category.sql: SELECT mc.name AS category, MIN(mi.base_price) AS min_price, MAX(mi.base_price) AS max_price, AVG(mi.base_price) AS avg_price FROM menu_items mi JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id WHERE mi.is_active = true GROUP BY mc.name ORDER BY avg_price DESC

items_never_ordered.description: Active menu items that have never been ordered
items_never_ordered.sql: SELECT mi.name, mi.base_price, mc.name AS category FROM menu_items mi JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id WHERE mi.is_active = true AND mi.id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT menu_item_id FROM order_items) ORDER BY mc.name

customers_with_orders.description: Number of customers who have placed at least one order
customers_with_orders.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL

repeat_customers.description: Number of customers who have placed more than one order
repeat_customers.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) sub

walkin_orders.description: Number of orders placed by walk-in customers (no account)
walkin_orders.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NULL

walkin_vs_registered_rate.description: Percentage split between walk-in and registered customer orders
walkin_vs_registered_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE customer_id IS NULL) / COUNT(*), 2) AS walkin_pct, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL) / COUNT(*), 2) AS registered_pct FROM orders

top_customers_by_spend.description: Top 10 customers by total spend on completed orders
top_customers_by_spend.sql: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10

customer_order_frequency.description: Average number of orders per customer
customer_order_frequency.sql: SELECT AVG(order_count) FROM (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY customer_id) sub

new_customers_by_month.description: Number of new customers registered per month (approximated by first order date)
new_customers_by_month.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', MIN(order_time)) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS new_customers FROM orders WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

total_loyalty_members.description: Total number of customers enrolled in the loyalty program

total_points_outstanding.description: Total loyalty points currently held by all customers

average_points_per_member.description: Average loyalty points balance per enrolled customer

total_points_earned.description: Total loyalty points ever earned across all customers
total_points_earned.sql: SELECT SUM(points_change) FROM loyalty_txn WHERE txn_type = 'earn'

total_points_redeemed.description: Total loyalty points ever redeemed across all customers
total_points_redeemed.sql: SELECT ABS(SUM(points_change)) FROM loyalty_txn WHERE txn_type = 'redeem'

redemption_rate.description: Percentage of earned points that have been redeemed
redemption_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * ABS(SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'redeem' THEN points_change ELSE 0 END)) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN txn_type = 'earn' THEN points_change ELSE 0 END), 0), 2) FROM loyalty_txn

top_loyalty_members.description: Top 10 customers by current loyalty points balance
top_loyalty_members.sql: SELECT c.name, la.points_balance FROM loyalty_accounts la JOIN customers c ON la.customer_id = c.id ORDER BY la.points_balance DESC LIMIT 10

payments.description: Payment methods, success rates and financial reconciliation

revenue_by_payment_method.description: Total revenue broken down by payment method
revenue_by_payment_method.sql: SELECT method, SUM(amount) AS total FROM payments WHERE status = 'paid' GROUP BY method ORDER BY total DESC

payment_success_rate.description: Percentage of payments that were successfully processed
payment_success_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'paid') / COUNT(*), 2) FROM payments

failed_payments.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM payments WHERE status = 'failed'

refunded_payments.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, SUM(amount) AS total_refunded FROM payments WHERE status = 'refunded'

average_payment_amount.description: Average payment amount per successful transaction
average_payment_amount.sql: SELECT AVG(amount) FROM payments WHERE status = 'paid'

discounts.description: Discount usage, impact and promotional effectiveness

total_discounts_applied.description: Total number of times discounts were applied to orders

most_used_discounts.description: Top 10 most frequently used discount codes
most_used_discounts.sql: SELECT d.code, d.description, COUNT(od.order_id) AS usage_count FROM order_discounts od JOIN discounts d ON od.discount_id = d.id GROUP BY d.code, d.description ORDER BY usage_count DESC LIMIT 10

active_discounts.description: Discount codes currently valid (within start and end date)
active_discounts.sql: SELECT code, description, type, amount FROM discounts WHERE (start_date IS NULL OR start_date <= CURRENT_DATE) AND (end_date IS NULL OR end_date >= CURRENT_DATE)

expired_discounts.sql: SELECT code, description, end_date FROM discounts WHERE end_date < CURRENT_DATE

inventory.description: Stock levels, reorder alerts and ingredient usage

low_stock_items.description: Ingredients at or below reorder threshold - need restocking
low_stock_items.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, i.name AS ingredient, inv.quantity_on_hand, inv.reorder_threshold FROM inventory inv JOIN ingredients i ON inv.ingredient_id = i.id JOIN location l ON inv.location_id = l.id WHERE inv.quantity_on_hand <= inv.reorder_threshold ORDER BY l.name, i.name

out_of_stock_items.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, i.name AS ingredient FROM inventory inv JOIN ingredients i ON inv.ingredient_id = i.id JOIN location l ON inv.location_id = l.id WHERE inv.quantity_on_hand = 0

inventory_by_location.description: Full inventory stock levels per location
inventory_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, i.name AS ingredient, inv.quantity_on_hand, i.unit FROM inventory inv JOIN ingredients i ON inv.ingredient_id = i.id JOIN location l ON inv.location_id = l.id ORDER BY l.name, i.name

stock_deficit.description: Ingredients below reorder threshold showing how much needs to be ordered
stock_deficit.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, i.name AS ingredient, inv.quantity_on_hand, inv.reorder_threshold, ROUND(inv.reorder_threshold - inv.quantity_on_hand, 2) AS deficit FROM inventory inv JOIN ingredients i ON inv.ingredient_id = i.id JOIN location l ON inv.location_id = l.id WHERE inv.quantity_on_hand < inv.reorder_threshold ORDER BY deficit DESC

total_ingredients.description: Total number of distinct ingredients tracked

total_employees.description: Total number of employees across all locations

employees_by_location.description: Number of employees per restaurant location
employees_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, COUNT(e.id) AS employee_count FROM employee e JOIN location l ON e.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY employee_count DESC

employees_by_role.description: Number of employees per job role
employees_by_role.sql: SELECT r.role_name, COUNT(e.id) AS count FROM employee e JOIN roles r ON e.role_id = r.id GROUP BY r.role_name ORDER BY count DESC

employees_by_role_per_location.description: Breakdown of employee roles per location
employees_by_role_per_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, r.role_name, COUNT(e.id) AS count FROM employee e JOIN roles r ON e.role_id = r.id JOIN location l ON e.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name, r.role_name ORDER BY l.name, count DESC

average_shift_duration_hours.description: Average duration of a work shift in hours
average_shift_duration_hours.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (shift_end - shift_start)) / 3600), 2) FROM shifts

shifts_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, COUNT(s.id) AS shift_count FROM shifts s JOIN location l ON s.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY shift_count DESC

upcoming_shifts.description: All shifts scheduled from now onwards
upcoming_shifts.sql: SELECT e.name AS employee, r.role_name, l.name AS location, s.shift_start, s.shift_end FROM shifts s JOIN employee e ON s.employee_id = e.id JOIN roles r ON e.role_id = r.id JOIN location l ON s.location_id = l.id WHERE s.shift_start >= NOW() ORDER BY s.shift_start

recently_hired_employees.description: Employees hired in the last 90 days
recently_hired_employees.sql: SELECT e.name, r.role_name, l.name AS location, e.hire_date FROM employee e JOIN roles r ON e.role_id = r.id JOIN location l ON e.location_id = l.id WHERE e.hire_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' ORDER BY e.hire_date DESC

total_shift_hours_by_employee.description: Total hours worked per employee based on scheduled shifts
total_shift_hours_by_employee.sql: SELECT e.name AS employee, ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.shift_end - s.shift_start)) / 3600), 2) AS total_hours FROM shifts s JOIN employee e ON s.employee_id = e.id GROUP BY e.name ORDER BY total_hours DESC

reservations.description: Table bookings, occupancy and no-show rates

reservations_by_status.description: Breakdown of reservations by status (booked, checked_in, no_show, cancelled)
reservations_by_status.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM reservations GROUP BY status ORDER BY count DESC

no_show_rate.description: Percentage of reservations that resulted in a no-show
no_show_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'no_show') / COUNT(*), 2) FROM reservations

checked_in_rate.description: Percentage of reservations that were successfully checked in
checked_in_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'checked_in') / COUNT(*), 2) FROM reservations

average_reservation_duration_hours.description: Average duration of a reservation in hours
average_reservation_duration_hours.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (end_time - start_time)) / 3600), 2) FROM reservations

table_utilisation.description: Number of reservations per table - identifies most and least used tables
table_utilisation.sql: SELECT t.table_number, l.name AS location, COUNT(r.id) AS reservation_count FROM reservations r JOIN tables t ON r.table_id = t.id JOIN location l ON t.location_id = l.id GROUP BY t.table_number, l.name ORDER BY reservation_count DESC

upcoming_reservations.description: All reservations scheduled from today onwards
upcoming_reservations.sql: SELECT c.name AS customer, t.table_number, l.name AS location, r.start_time, r.end_time, r.status FROM reservations r JOIN customers c ON r.customer_id = c.id JOIN tables t ON r.table_id = t.id JOIN location l ON t.location_id = l.id WHERE r.start_time >= NOW() ORDER BY r.start_time

reservations_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, COUNT(r.id) AS reservation_count FROM reservations r JOIN tables t ON r.table_id = t.id JOIN location l ON t.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY reservation_count DESC

orders_by_location.description: Total orders placed at each location
orders_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN location l ON o.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY order_count DESC

revenue_per_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN location l ON o.location_id = l.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY revenue DESC

tables_by_location.description: Number of dining tables per location
tables_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, COUNT(t.id) AS table_count FROM tables t JOIN location l ON t.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY table_count DESC

total_seating_capacity.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, SUM(t.capacity) AS total_seats FROM tables t JOIN location l ON t.location_id = l.id GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY total_seats DESC

revenue_per_seat.description: Revenue efficiency per seat - total revenue divided by seating capacity per location
revenue_per_seat.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, ROUND(SUM(o.total_amount) / NULLIF(SUM(t.capacity), 0), 2) AS revenue_per_seat FROM orders o JOIN location l ON o.location_id = l.id JOIN tables t ON t.location_id = l.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY revenue_per_seat DESC

average_order_value_by_location.description: Average order value per location for completed orders
average_order_value_by_location.sql: SELECT l.name AS location, ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders o JOIN location l ON o.location_id = l.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY l.name ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC

recipes.description: Recipe composition and ingredient requirements per menu item

total_recipes.description: Total number of menu items with defined recipes

ingredients_per_dish.description: Number of ingredients required per menu item
ingredients_per_dish.sql: SELECT mi.name AS menu_item, COUNT(ri.ingredient_id) AS ingredient_count FROM recipe_items ri JOIN menu_items mi ON ri.menu_item_id = mi.id GROUP BY mi.name ORDER BY ingredient_count DESC

most_used_ingredients.description: Ingredients used across the most menu items
most_used_ingredients.sql: SELECT i.name AS ingredient, i.unit, COUNT(ri.menu_item_id) AS used_in_dishes FROM recipe_items ri JOIN ingredients i ON ri.ingredient_id = i.id GROUP BY i.name, i.unit ORDER BY used_in_dishes DESC

recipe_details.description: Full recipe breakdown showing all ingredients and quantities per dish
recipe_details.sql: SELECT mi.name AS menu_item, mc.name AS category, i.name AS ingredient, ri.qty_per_recipe, i.unit FROM recipe_items ri JOIN menu_items mi ON ri.menu_item_id = mi.id JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id JOIN ingredients i ON ri.ingredient_id = i.id ORDER BY mi.name, i.name

menu_items_without_recipe.description: Active menu items that have no recipe defined
menu_items_without_recipe.sql: SELECT mi.name, mc.name AS category FROM menu_items mi JOIN menu_categories mc ON mi.category_id = mc.id WHERE mi.is_active = true AND mi.id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT menu_item_id FROM recipe_items) ORDER BY mc.name

suppliers.description: Supplier management, ingredient pricing and procurement

ingredients_per_supplier.description: Number of ingredients each supplier provides
ingredients_per_supplier.sql: SELECT s.name AS supplier, COUNT(si.ingredient_id) AS ingredient_count FROM supplier_items si JOIN supplier s ON si.supplier_id = s.id GROUP BY s.name ORDER BY ingredient_count DESC

ingredient_cost_by_supplier.description: Cost per ingredient from each supplier - useful for procurement decisions
ingredient_cost_by_supplier.sql: SELECT s.name AS supplier, i.name AS ingredient, si.unit_cost, i.unit FROM supplier_items si JOIN supplier s ON si.supplier_id = s.id JOIN ingredients i ON si.ingredient_id = i.id ORDER BY i.name, si.unit_cost

cheapest_supplier_per_ingredient.description: Lowest cost supplier for each ingredient
cheapest_supplier_per_ingredient.sql: SELECT i.name AS ingredient, s.name AS supplier, si.unit_cost, i.unit FROM supplier_items si JOIN supplier s ON si.supplier_id = s.id JOIN ingredients i ON si.ingredient_id = i.id WHERE si.unit_cost = (SELECT MIN(unit_cost) FROM supplier_items WHERE ingredient_id = si.ingredient_id) ORDER BY i.name

supplier_price_comparison.description: Side-by-side price comparison for ingredients available from multiple suppliers
supplier_price_comparison.sql: SELECT i.name AS ingredient, i.unit, COUNT(si.supplier_id) AS supplier_count, MIN(si.unit_cost) AS min_price, MAX(si.unit_cost) AS max_price, ROUND(AVG(si.unit_cost), 2) AS avg_price FROM supplier_items si JOIN ingredients i ON si.ingredient_id = i.id GROUP BY i.name, i.unit HAVING COUNT(si.supplier_id) > 1 ORDER BY (MAX(si.unit_cost) - MIN(si.unit_cost)) DESC

ingredients_with_single_supplier.description: Ingredients sourced from only one supplier - supply chain risk
ingredients_with_single_supplier.sql: SELECT i.name AS ingredient, s.name AS supplier, si.unit_cost, i.unit FROM supplier_items si JOIN ingredients i ON si.ingredient_id = i.id JOIN supplier s ON si.supplier_id = s.id WHERE i.id IN (SELECT ingredient_id FROM supplier_items GROUP BY ingredient_id HAVING COUNT(*) = 1) ORDER BY i.name

business_rules: Completed orders: status = 'completed' - use this filter for all revenue calculations
Active menu items: is_active = true - only these are available for ordering
Walk-in customers: orders.customer_id IS NULL - these have no customer profile
Loyalty points earned: loyalty_txn.txn_type = 'earn' and points_change > 0
Loyalty points redeemed: loyalty_txn.txn_type = 'redeem' and points_change < 0
Payment amount includes total_amount + tax_amount + tip_amount
Low stock: inventory.quantity_on_hand <= inventory.reorder_threshold
Active discounts: start_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND end_date >= CURRENT_DATE
Percentage discounts: discounts.type = 'percent'
Fixed amount discounts: discounts.type = 'fixed'
sql_patterns: Revenue/order queries: always filter WHERE status = 'completed'
Active menu items: always filter WHERE is_active = true
Loyalty points earned: WHERE txn_type = 'earn' AND points_change > 0
Loyalty points redeemed: WHERE txn_type = 'redeem' AND points_change < 0
Active discounts: WHERE start_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND end_date >= CURRENT_DATE
Walk-in customers: orders.customer_id IS NULL (no registered account)
Registered customers: orders.customer_id IS NOT NULL

join_notes.payments->orders: one-to-one: each order has exactly one payment
join_notes.order_items->orders: use with WHERE status='completed' for revenue calculations
join_notes.loyalty_txn->loyalty_accounts: points history chain: loyalty_txn -> loyalty_accounts -> customers
join_notes.supplier_items->ingredients: pricing per ingredient from each supplier

admin.description: Full system access - all domains, all data, no restrictions
business_rules: Full access to all tables including supplier pricing and recipe costs
Can view all customer PII including email, phone, address
Can view all locations data without restriction

general_manager.description: Cross-location management - all domains except supplier procurement, PII restricted
business_rules: Cannot access supplier pricing or recipe ingredient costs - supplier and supplier_items tables are not available
Customer and employee PII (email, phone, address) is hidden - use name only
Can compare performance across all locations
Revenue means total completed order revenue across all locations
Inventory metrics available: low_stock_items, out_of_stock_items, inventory_by_location only

location_manager.description: Single location management - location-scoped data only
business_rules: ALL queries are automatically filtered to the manager's assigned location
Revenue means revenue for this location only - never cross-location totals
Staff means employees assigned to this location only
Inventory means stock at this location only
Cannot access loyalty program, discount management or supplier data

chef.description: Kitchen operations - menu, recipes and inventory only
business_rules: Can only access menu items, recipe ingredients and inventory stock levels
Cannot access any financial data, revenue, payments or customer information
Cannot access staff schedules, shifts or employee data
Active menu items means is_active = true
Low stock means quantity_on_hand <= reorder_threshold

waiter.description: Floor operations - orders, tables, reservations and menu only
business_rules: Can view current orders, table status and today's reservations
Cannot access financial totals, revenue, payment amounts or tips - do NOT query total_amount, tax_amount, tip_amount
Cannot access inventory, staff data or loyalty program details
Customer contact details (email, phone, address) are hidden
Active menu items means is_active = true - only show available dishes

cashier.description: Payment processing - orders, payments and discounts only
business_rules: Can view orders and order items to process payments
Can apply discount codes to orders
Payment amount = total_amount + tax_amount + tip_amount
Active discounts: start_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND end_date >= CURRENT_DATE
Cannot access customer profiles, loyalty accounts or inventory
Cannot access staff data, shifts or location management
Failed payments: status = 'failed', Refunded: status = 'refunded'

analyst.description: Read-only analytics - all aggregated data, no PII, no supplier costs
business_rules: Read-only access - never suggest INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE or DROP
All customer and employee PII is hidden - use aggregated data only
Cannot access supplier pricing or recipe costs
Revenue always means completed orders: status = 'completed'
Always use aggregations (SUM, AVG, COUNT) - never return raw customer records
Trend analysis: use DATE_TRUNC for monthly/weekly grouping
